Title: Right click context menu
Summary: Bleepforge replaces the browser context menu globally. Components can register their own items.
Body:
## What you get by default

Right click on text and the menu shows Cut, Copy, Paste based on what is appropriate for the target. On editable inputs the Paste path uses a native value setter so React's onChange fires, keeping controlled components in sync.

## Component owned menus

Specific surfaces own their menu and replace the default:

- [Dialog graph](/dialogs) sequence nodes show "Edit" and "Copy id".
- [Dialog graph](/dialogs) canvas (empty space) shows "Create new sequence".
- [Asset gallery](/assets) cards show "Edit", "Duplicate", "Delete", "Preview" depending on the surface (the [click vs right click rule](/help/assets-and-image-editor/click-vs-right-click) controls which items appear where).

## Implementation note

The listeners use the capture phase. Without that, the React Flow pane in the dialog graph would swallow the mousedown event on its own `stopPropagation` and the menu would never get a chance to show.

When the cursor is in the bottom 30% of the viewport, the menu anchors by its bottom edge instead of its top edge so right clicks near the page bottom never produce a clipped menu.
